title: Butter Chicken
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Madhur Jaffrey-style butter chicken: a spiced tomato, yoghurt and butter sauce with tender chicken for TM6 and TM7.
intro: Madhur Jaffrey made Indian home cooking feel generous, layered and doable. This Thermomix version builds a proper spiced tomato base first, then cooks the chicken gently on Reverse so the sauce is silky and the pieces stay intact.
ingredients: 600 g chicken thigh fillets, cut into 3 cm pieces
Fresh coriander and basmati rice, to serve
steps: In a bowl, toss chicken with yoghurt, lemon juice, 1 tsp garam masala, cumin, coriander, paprika and chilli. Marinate 20 min / 0°C / no speed while you make the sauce.
Place onion, garlic and ginger in the mixing bowl and chop 5 sec / 0°C / speed 6. Scrape down.
Add ghee and sauté 5 min / 120°C / speed 1, MC off, until the aromatics smell sweet and cooked.
Add crushed tomatoes, salt and the remaining 1 tsp garam masala. Cook 10 min / 100°C / speed 2, MC off with the simmering basket on the lid.
Blend the sauce 30 sec / 0°C / speed 7, increasing gradually, until smooth.
Add marinated chicken. Cook 14 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed soft, MC on, until the chicken is cooked through.
Add cream and extra butter. Warm 2 min / 90°C / Reverse / speed 1, then rest 3 min before serving with coriander and rice.
tips: Chicken thighs stay juicier than breast in a Thermomix curry.
Blend the tomato sauce before adding chicken — that gives the proper butter-chicken texture without shredding the meat.
For a deeper sauce, marinate the chicken in the fridge for 2–6 hours before cooking.

faq.q: Can I make butter chicken in a Thermomix without shredding the chicken?
faq.a: Yes. Blend the sauce first, then add chicken pieces and cook on Reverse / soft speed so the chicken stays chunky.
You can, but reduce the chicken cooking time to about 11–12 minutes and check doneness so it does not dry out.

faq.q: Is this the original Madhur Jaffrey recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Madhur Jaffrey and butter chicken; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
